# Coronium Reseller Kit

Open-source starter kit for building a mobile-proxy reseller dashboard on top of the Coronium API (<https://dashboard.coronium.io>).

## What this is for

You (the LLM agent) are scaffolding a proxy reseller business for someone. They sell Coronium's mobile 4G/5G proxies to their end-customers with markup. This repo gives you the pattern, examples, and exact API surface to do it in one shot.

## Where to start reading

1. <https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bolivian-peru/coronium-reseller-kit/main/AGENTS.md> — your instructions
2. <https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bolivian-peru/coronium-reseller-kit/main/PROMPT.md> — the prompts the human will paste
3. <https://github.com/bolivian-peru/coronium-reseller-kit/tree/main/examples/nextjs-dashboard> — working foundation
4. <https://dashboard.coronium.io/api-docs/> — authoritative interactive API reference (OpenAPI / Swagger UI, public)

## API endpoints you'll use

- `GET https://api.coronium.io/api/v3/account/proxies` — list owned proxies
- `GET https://api.coronium.io/api/v3/account/proxies/health` — per-proxy liveness
- `POST https://api.coronium.io/api/v3/payment/buy-modems-with-crypto-balance` — buy proxies
- `POST https://api.coronium.io/api/v3/modems/{id}/restart` — rotate IP. Append `?sync=true` to block ≤25 s and return real outcome (`200` with `new_ip`, `502 rotation_failed`, or `503 rotation_timeout`) instead of fire-and-forget 200.
- `POST https://api.coronium.io/api/v3/modems/{id}/replace` — swap broken proxy
- `PUT https://api.coronium.io/api/v3/account/webhook` — register webhook URL

Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header. JWT lifetime 365 days.

## Critical patterns

- **Customer mapping**: use the freeform `metadata` JSON field on each modem to track which of the reseller's end-customers owns which proxy. Don't create a separate database table.
- **Server-side JWT**: never put `CORONIUM_API_KEY` in client-side code. All API calls go through the reseller's own server.
- **Webhook auto-swap**: when a customer's modem dies, Coronium fires `modem.replaced` to the reseller's webhook URL with old + new modem IDs and full credentials. Update local mapping, notify end-customer.

## Don't

- Don't reinvent auto-swap or health-check logic — the backend does it.
- Don't put JWT in browser.
- Don't create a sidecar `modems` table — the metadata field is the customer-mapping layer.
- Don't retry-loop on `409 stock_unavailable` — pick a different country instead.
